script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'chia-audit',
  description: 'Security audit of chia-blockchain high-value remote-reachable surface: P2P handlers, mempool/consensus, wallet RPC + offers, data_layer. Find -> adversarial verify -> rank.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Find', detail: 'deep-read finders per remote-reachable subsystem' },
    { title: 'Verify', detail: 'adversarial refute-by-default panel' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'rank by severity x reachability x program payout' },
  ],
}

const ROOT = `C:/bugBounty/targets/chia-blockchain`

const THREAT = [
  `THREAT MODEL (chia full node / wallet):`,
  `- HIGHEST value: an UNTRUSTED remote P2P peer sending crafted protocol messages to a full node (no auth; anyone can connect). Goal classes: remote DoS (unbounded alloc/CPU, decompression, infinite loop), consensus/validation bypass, memory/logic errors, peer-data injection.`,
  `- RPC clients: the wallet/full_node RPC and the daemon websocket. Sometimes reachable beyond localhost or cross-origin. Goal: missing/weak auth or origin checks, SSRF, parameter injection, command injection, key/secret disclosure.`,
  `- Wallet users / counterparties: offer/trade files and CATs/NFTs from other users. Goal: offer manipulation enabling fund theft, signature/spend-bundle validation gaps, integer/amount handling.`,
  `- data_layer HTTP server/clients. Goal: path traversal, SSRF, auth bypass, arbitrary file read/write.`,
  `Prefer findings reachable by a remote/untrusted actor with no or low privilege.`,
].join('\n')

const FINDINGS_SCHEMA = { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, properties:{
  findings:{ type:'array', items:{ type:'object', additionalProperties:false, properties:{
    title:{type:'string'}, file:{type:'string'}, line:{type:'integer'}, vuln_class:{type:'string'}, cwe:{type:'string'},
    severity:{type:'string', enum:['Critical','High','Medium','Low','Info']},
    source:{type:'string'}, sink:{type:'string'}, data_flow:{type:'string'}, preconditions:{type:'string'},
    exploitability:{type:'string'}, confidence:{type:'number'}, code_excerpt:{type:'string'} },
    required:['title','file','vuln_class','severity','source','sink','data_flow','confidence'] } },
  coverage_notes:{type:'string'} }, required:['findings'] }

const VERDICT_SCHEMA = { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, properties:{
  is_real:{type:'boolean'}, refuted:{type:'boolean'}, reason:{type:'string'},
  attacker_controlled:{type:'boolean'}, sink_reachable:{type:'boolean'}, mitigation_present:{type:'boolean'},
  adjusted_severity:{type:'string'}, exploit_sketch:{type:'string'}, confidence:{type:'number'} },
  required:['is_real','refuted','reason','confidence'] }

const REPORT_SCHEMA = { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, properties:{
  executive_summary:{type:'string'},
  ranked_findings:{ type:'array', items:{ type:'object', additionalProperties:false, properties:{
    rank:{type:'integer'}, title:{type:'string'}, file:{type:'string'}, line:{type:'integer'}, vuln_class:{type:'string'},
    cwe:{type:'string'}, severity:{type:'string'}, cvss_vector:{type:'string'}, why_high_value:{type:'string'},
    exploitability:{type:'string'}, program_payout_note:{type:'string'}, suggested_next_step:{type:'string'}, confidence:{type:'number'} },
    required:['rank','title','severity','why_high_value','suggested_next_step'] } },
  program_scope_notes:{type:'string'}, recommended_priority_order:{type:'array', items:{type:'string'}} },
  required:['executive_summary','ranked_findings'] }

const CLUSTERS = [
  { key:'fullnode-p2p', base:`${ROOT}/chia/full_node`,
    hint:'Full node P2P message handlers reachable by any peer. Read full_node_api.py (each @api_request handler) and full_node.py. Trace peer-supplied fields into processing. Hunt remote DoS (unbounded loops/allocations on peer input, missing size/rate limits), unvalidated peer data used in state, and exceptions that crash. Also weight_proof.py / sync logic if present.',
    classes:'remote DoS, peer-data injection, unhandled-exception crash, resource exhaustion' },
  { key:'mempool-consensus', base:`${ROOT}/chia/full_node`,
    hint:'Mempool + consensus validation. Read mempool_manager.py, mempool.py, and pull in chia/consensus/*.py (block_body_validation, blockchain, cost/fee logic). Hunt: spend-bundle/transaction validation gaps, fee/cost miscalculation, double-spend or replacement bugs, consensus rule that a crafted block/tx can bypass, integer overflow in amounts/cost.',
    classes:'consensus/validation bypass, double-spend, fee/cost manipulation, integer overflow' },
  { key:'wallet-rpc', base:`${ROOT}/chia/wallet`,
    hint:'Wallet RPC API. Read wallet_rpc_api.py in full and supporting wallet_state_manager.py. Hunt: endpoints lacking auth/validation, parameter injection, operations that move funds or sign without proper checks, key/mnemonic/secret disclosure through responses or logs, path/file params.',
    classes:'broken auth, key/secret disclosure, parameter injection, unsafe fund operation' },
  { key:'wallet-offers', base:`${ROOT}/chia/wallet/trading`,
    hint:'Offers/trades (cross-user financial data). Read offer.py, trade_manager.py, and CAT/NFT spend construction it relies on. Hunt: a malicious offer/trade file that validates but steals funds or mismatches amounts, missing checks on counterparty-supplied spend bundles, signature/announcement validation gaps, royalty/amount manipulation.',
    classes:'fund theft via crafted offer, spend-bundle validation gap, amount/royalty manipulation' },
  { key:'data-layer', base:`${ROOT}/chia/data_layer`,
    hint:'Data layer (DL) HTTP server/clients and store. Read data_layer.py, data_store.py, download_data/server files, dl_wallet if present. Hunt: path traversal in file/key handling, SSRF in URL fetch of remote DL data, auth bypass on the HTTP server, arbitrary file read/write, unsafe extraction.',
    classes:'path traversal, SSRF, broken auth, arbitrary file read/write' },
  { key:'rpc-daemon', base:`${ROOT}/chia/rpc`,
    hint:'RPC server core + daemon. Read chia/rpc/rpc_server.py, chia/rpc/util.py, chia/daemon/server.py, chia/daemon/keychain_server.py. Hunt (deeper than a surface pass): websocket/RPC origin & auth validation, whether the daemon can be driven by a local web page (CSWSH) or non-localhost client, command/argument injection when launching services/plotters, keychain access without authorization.',
    classes:'missing/weak auth, cross-site websocket hijack, command injection, keychain access bypass' },
  { key:'server-tls', base:`${ROOT}/chia/server`,
    hint:'P2P networking + TLS. Read server.py, ssl_context.py, ws_connection.py, node_discovery.py, address_manager.py, upnp.py, rate_limits*.py. Hunt: certificate/identity validation weaknesses (peer auth), message framing/size limits (DoS), peer-address handling, UPnP abuse, rate-limit bypass.',
    classes:'improper peer/cert validation, remote DoS, peer-address poisoning, rate-limit bypass' },
]

function finderPrompt(c){ return [
  `You are a senior security auditor reviewing the chia-blockchain node for its bug-bounty program. Rigorous, skeptical, concrete data-flow required. An earlier pass over daemon/server/rpc found nothing easy — go deeper.`,
  `REPO ROOT: ${ROOT}. PRIMARY DIR: ${c.base} (read relevant .py in full, recurse). Use Grep/Glob to follow handlers, decorators (@api_request), call chains, and the protocol message definitions in chia/protocols/*.`,
  ``, THREAT, ``,
  `CLUSTER FOCUS: ${c.hint}`,
  `CLASSES: ${c.classes}`,
  ``,
  `METHOD: find the dangerous operations; trace BACKWARD to an untrusted source (peer message field, RPC param, offer file, HTTP request); check for validation/limits/auth/mitigations in between; only report when a remote/low-priv actor can reach it. Severity = impact x reachability (remote unauth = highest).`,
  `At most 6 findings, best-first. Zero is an acceptable honest answer. Each: exact file:line, source, sink, concrete data_flow with function names, preconditions, exploitability, honest confidence in [0,1]. Return ONLY the structured object.`,
].join('\n') }

function verifyPrompt(f, lens){
  const head=[`Adversarial reviewer: REFUTE this candidate; default refuted=true unless solid. Read the real chia code at ${ROOT} first.`,``,
    `CANDIDATE: ${f.title}`, `class: ${f.vuln_class} (${f.cwe||'cwe?'}) severity: ${f.severity}`,
    `file: ${f.file}:${f.line||'?'}`, `source: ${f.source}`, `sink: ${f.sink}`, `data_flow: ${f.data_flow}`, `excerpt: ${f.code_excerpt||'(none)'}`, ``]
  const lensText={
    reachability:`LENS = REACHABILITY & CONTROL. Is the source truly reachable/controllable by an untrusted remote or low-priv actor in a default node config (peer connection accepted? RPC exposed/authless? handler actually registered)? Is the sink reached (not dead code, not gated behind operator-only config)? If trusted-only or unreachable, refuted=true.`,
    mitigation:`LENS = MITIGATION. Look for validation, size/rate limits, auth/origin checks, exception handling that contains impact, parameterization, allow-lists between source and sink. If an effective mitigation defeats it, refuted=true.`,
    repro:`LENS = CONCRETE EXPLOIT. Construct the exact message/request/offer and the resulting impact. If you cannot build a plausible exploit from the real code, refuted=true.`,
  }[lens]
  return [...head, lensText, ``, `is_real=true only if it survives against the real code. Provide exploit_sketch and adjusted_severity if real. Return ONLY the structured object.`].join('\n')
}

const results = await pipeline(CLUSTERS,
  (c)=>agent(finderPrompt(c),{label:`find:${c.key}`,phase:'Find',schema:FINDINGS_SCHEMA}),
  (review,c)=>{ const fs=(review&&review.findings)?review.findings:[]
    return parallel(fs.map((f)=>()=>{ const heavy=f.severity==='Critical'||f.severity==='High'
      const lenses=heavy?['reachability','mitigation','repro']:['repro']
      return parallel(lenses.map((l)=>()=>agent(verifyPrompt(f,l),{label:`verify:${c.key}:${l}`,phase:'Verify',schema:VERDICT_SCHEMA})))
        .then((votes)=>{ const v=votes.filter(Boolean); const real=v.filter(x=>x.is_real&&!x.refuted).length
          return {...f, cluster:c.key, verifiers:v, votes_real:real, votes_total:v.length, survives:v.length>0&&real>=Math.ceil(v.length/2)} }) })) })

const all = results.flat().filter(Boolean)
const confirmed = all.filter(f=>f.survives)
log(`chia candidates: ${all.length} | confirmed: ${confirmed.length}`)

phase('Synthesize')
const synthInput = confirmed.map(f=>({ title:f.title, file:f.file, line:f.line, vuln_class:f.vuln_class, cwe:f.cwe,
  severity:f.severity, source:f.source, sink:f.sink, data_flow:f.data_flow, exploitability:f.exploitability,
  votes:`${f.votes_real}/${f.votes_total}`,
  verifier_notes:(f.verifiers||[]).map(v=>({real:v.is_real,refuted:v.refuted,reachable:v.attacker_controlled,mitig:v.mitigation_present,sev:v.adjusted_severity,sketch:v.exploit_sketch,reason:v.reason})) }))

const report = await agent([
  `You are a bug-bounty lead reporting to the Chia Network program (HackerOne). Findings below survived an adversarial panel. Produce a prioritized, payout-aware report.`, ``,
  `INSTRUCTIONS: rank by severity x reachability x payout. Assign CVSS:3.1 vectors. Use web search to confirm the Chia Network HackerOne program is active and its rough payout tiers; put conclusions in program_scope_notes (say if a tool is unavailable). Be conservative and defensible; if empty, record the null result and name the deepest areas to revisit. For each finding give why_high_value, a concrete suggested_next_step (PoC/repro), and program_payout_note.`, ``,
  `FINDINGS JSON:`, JSON.stringify(synthInput,null,2),
].join('\n'), {label:'synthesize', phase:'Synthesize', schema:REPORT_SCHEMA})

return { candidate_count:all.length, confirmed_count:confirmed.length, confirmed, report }

confirmed.title: SSRF: DataLayer node fetches attacker-controlled mirror URLs taken from unauthenticated on-chain coin spends
confirmed.source: On-chain CREATE_COIN memos of a mirror coin (P2_PARENT.curry(1) puzzle, no owner/signature check). Parsed in chia/wallet/db_wallet/db_wallet_puzzles.py:get_mirror_info() lines 107-109 (memos[1:] -> url list) and stored unvalidated by chia/data_layer/data_layer_wallet.py:coin_added() lines 780-795 via Mirror.decode_urls().
confirmed.sink: aiohttp ClientSession.get(server_info.url + "/" + filename, proxy=proxy_url) in http_download(), chia/data_layer/download_data.py lines 313-318.
confirmed.data_flow: Any party spends a coin on-chain creating a mirror coin (create_mirror_puzzle, anyone-can-create; amount can be minimal) whose CREATE_COIN memos encode launcher_id + arbitrary URL strings. When a victim DL node tracks that launcher_id (it tracks every store it subscribes to via DataLayer.subscribe -> dl_track_new, or that it owns), DataLayerWallet.coin_added() (data_layer_wallet.py:774-796) sees the mirror coin, calls get_mirror_info() to extract the URLs with zero validation, and stores them via dl_store.add_mirror(). The periodic DataLayer.periodically_manage_data() loop runs DataLayer.update_subscription() (data_layer.py:1076-1089) for every subscription, which calls update_subscriptions_from_wallet() (data_layer.py:953-959): it pulls all mirrors via wallet_rpc.dl_get_mirrors and writes their URLs straight into the subscriptions table (data_store.update_subscriptions_from_wallet, data_store.py:1645). It then calls fetch_and_validate() (data_layer.py:607-687) which reads those URLs via get_available_servers_for_store() and, for each, calls insert_from_delta_file() -> download_file() (download_data.py:110-145, with downloader None for plain HTTP) -> http_download() (download_data.py:298-318) issuing aiohttp GET to server_info.url + '/' + filename. No scheme allowlist (http/https only enforced implicitly by aiohttp), no private/link-local IP filtering, no DNS-rebinding protection, and aiohttp follows redirects by default. filename is constrained (bytes32 hex + fixed suffix) but the host/scheme/path-prefix of the URL are fully attacker-controlled.
confirmed.preconditions: Victim runs the data_layer service and is subscribed to (or owns) a store whose launcher_id the attacker targets. Attacker only needs the ability to create one mirror coin on-chain for that launcher_id (no auth, no relationship to the victim). The node must be in a state where fetch_and_validate attempts a download (i.e. local generation behind chain generation), which is the normal sync condition; even when synced the URL is persisted and used on the next lagging generation.
confirmed.exploitability: Remote, unauthenticated on-chain actor. Largely blind SSRF over HTTP(S): the node will issue GET requests to internal addresses (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/... cloud metadata, internal admin HTTP services, localhost-only ports), follow redirects, and the downloaded body is written to a size-capped file (max_delta_file_size, default 250 MiB) rather than returned to the attacker. Impact is internal-service request forgery / internal port reachability / metadata-endpoint probing and forced outbound connections from the node, plus a controllable target for failed-download bookkeeping. Not arbitrary file read (aiohttp does not honor file://).
confirmed.code_excerpt:
async with session.get(
    server_info.url + "/" + filename,
    headers=headers,
    timeout=timeout,
    proxy=proxy_url,
) as resp:

verifiers.exploit_sketch:
Blind SSRF via unauthenticated on-chain mirror coins. The DataLayer mirror puzzle create_mirror_puzzle() = P2_PARENT.curry(1) (chia/wallet/db_wallet/db_wallet_puzzles.py:90-94) is anyone-can-create with no owner key or signature, by design (mirrors are permissionless host announcements).

Concrete exploit:
1. Victim runs a chia data_layer service and has subscribed to some store launcher_id L (the normal DL use case). subscribe() -> wallet_rpc.dl_track_new() inserts L into singleton_records, so is_launcher_tracked(L) returns True (dl_wallet_store.py:293-302).
2. Attacker spends ANY coin they control on-chain, producing a CREATE_COIN to MIRROR_PUZZLE_HASH with a small amount (e.g. 1 mojo). The spend's CREATE_COIN memo list is [L, b"http://169.254.169.254:80", ...] -- launcher_id followed by arbitrary URL byte strings. No signature tying the spend to L's owner is required.
3. The victim wallet observes the new mirror coin. DataLayerWallet.coin_added() (data_layer_wallet.py:773-796) fetches the parent spend, runs get_mirror_info() (db_wallet_puzzles.py:97-110) which returns (L, memos[1:]) with zero validation, checks only is_launcher_tracked(L)==True, and stores Mirror.decode_urls(urls) (plain utf-8 decode, no checks) via dl_store.add_mirror().
4. On the next periodically_manage_data() cycle (default 60s), update_subscription() -> update_subscriptions_from_wallet() (data_layer.py:953-959) pulls the attacker URLs via dl_get_mirrors and writes them straight into the subscriptions table.
5. fetch_and_validate() (data_layer.py:607+) reads them through get_available_servers_for_store() and calls insert_from_delta_file() -> download_file() (downloader=None for plain http, so http_download) -> http_download() (download_data.py:298-318) issues aiohttp session.get(server_info.url + "/" + filename). filename is the fixed-format "{store_id}-{node_hash}-delta-{gen}-v1.0.dat"; the scheme/host/port/path-prefix of server_info.url are fully attacker-controlled.

No scheme allowlist, no private/link-local/loopback IP filtering, no DNS-rebinding protection exist anywhere in the path (grep for urlparse/is_private/169.254/scheme over download_data.py + data_layer.py + data_store.py returns nothing). aiohttp follows redirects by default (allow_redirects not set), so an attacker URL can 30x-redirect to any internal target, further widening reach.

Impact = blind/semi-blind SSRF: the node can be coerced into GET requests to arbitrary internal/cloud-metadata endpoints (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data path-prefix attacker-set, full path reachable via redirect). Response bodies are NOT reflected to the attacker, and downloaded delta-file content is later hash-validated against on-chain root hashes, so no data can be injected into the store. max_delta_file_size caps download size. So the value is internal port-scanning / hitting internal HTTP services / metadata exfil only if the target service leaks via timing/error behavior, not direct data return.

Limiting factors keeping this at Medium rather than High: (a) requires the victim to be subscribed to / own a store launcher_id (is_launcher_tracked gate) -- though that is the routine DataLayer state and the attacker can self-publish a store to lure subscribers; (b) blind (no response reflection); (c) path suffix is constrained to the .dat filename format absent a redirect.
verifiers.reason: Survives against the real code as a genuine blind SSRF. Source is unauthenticated and fully attacker-controlled: create_mirror_puzzle() = P2_PARENT.curry(1) is anyone-can-create with no signature, and get_mirror_info() (db_wallet_puzzles.py:107-109) + Mirror.decode_urls() (data_layer_wallet.py:82-83) extract memo URLs with zero validation. coin_added() (data_layer_wallet.py:773-796) stores them gated only by is_launcher_tracked(launcher_id). The flow to the sink is intact: periodically_manage_data -> update_subscription -> update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:953-959) -> fetch_and_validate (data_layer.py:607+) -> insert_from_delta_file -> download_file (downloader None) -> http_download (download_data.py:313-318) issuing aiohttp GET to server_info.url. I confirmed NO scheme allowlist, NO private/link-local IP filtering, and NO DNS-rebinding protection anywhere in the download path; aiohttp follows redirects by default. The host/scheme/port are attacker-controlled; only the .dat path suffix is fixed. Severity stays Medium (not higher) because it is blind: response bodies are never returned to the attacker, downloaded content is hash-validated against on-chain roots so no store-data injection is possible, max_delta_file_size caps the fetch, and exploitation requires the victim to be tracking the launcher_id (the normal subscribed state, and the attacker can self-publish a luring store, so this is a soft gate). The candidate's data_flow, source/sink line references, and 'no validation' claims all check out against the real chia code.

confirmed.title: SSRF: subscribe / add_mirror RPC accept arbitrary URLs that the node later fetches, with no scheme or host validation
confirmed.source: RPC request fields: data_layer_rpc_api.py subscribe() line 369 (request['urls']) and add_mirror() line 471 (request['urls']).
confirmed.sink: Same http_download() GET at chia/data_layer/download_data.py:313-318, reached via DataLayer.subscribe() (data_layer.py:869-876, only url.rstrip('/') applied) -> data_store.subscribe -> fetch_and_validate -> http_download.
confirmed.data_flow: DataLayerRpcApi.subscribe (data_layer_rpc_api.py:360-371) passes request['urls'] unchanged to DataLayer.subscribe (data_layer.py:869), which only does url.rstrip('/') and stores ServerInfo(url,0,0). The periodic manage loop then fetches each URL via http_download exactly as in finding 1. add_mirror (data_layer.py:939-944) likewise forwards arbitrary urls to the wallet to be published on-chain. No scheme/host/IP validation anywhere before the outbound GET.
confirmed.preconditions: Attacker can reach the DataLayer RPC endpoint (rpc_port). Normally bound to self_hostname with TLS client-cert mutual auth, so this requires the RPC to be exposed beyond localhost or the client cert/key to be reachable - a lower-likelihood precondition than the on-chain vector. Provided to document the missing input validation that makes finding 1 trivially weaponizable once any URL reaches the subscriptions table.
confirmed.exploitability: Same blind-HTTP SSRF primitive as finding 1 but requires RPC access (auth/localhost), hence Low for the remote-unauth threat model.
confirmed.code_excerpt:
async def subscribe(self, store_id: bytes32, urls: list[str]) -> Subscription:
    parsed_urls = [url.rstrip("/") for url in urls]
    subscription = Subscription(store_id, [ServerInfo(url, 0, 0) for url in parsed_urls])

verifiers.reason:
Data flow verified against real code and survives. subscribe RPC (data_layer_rpc_api.py:360-371, request['urls']) -> DataLayer.subscribe (data_layer.py:869-876) applies only url.rstrip('/'), stores ServerInfo, and the periodic loop (periodically_manage_data -> update_subscription -> fetch_and_validate, data_layer.py:1076-1085, 607-675) feeds server_info.url into http_download (download_data.py:298-318) which does session.get(server_info.url + '/' + filename). No scheme/host/IP/loopback/private-range validation exists at any hop -- confirmed. So the SSRF mechanism is real.

Two threat-model nuances refine the severity rather than refute it:
1) DIRECT RPC vector is operator-only. The DataLayer RPC server uses ssl_context_for_server with verify_mode=ssl.CERT_REQUIRED against the node's PRIVATE CA (server.py:67-72) and binds to self_hostname. An external party cannot call subscribe/add_mirror directly; doing so requires the node's own RPC client cert. Operator-pointing-own-node is self-SSRF (negligible).
2) The genuinely remote vector is the ON-CHAIN MIRROR path: update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:953-959) pulls mirror URLs via dl_get_mirrors -- those are mirror coins anyone can publish on-chain for any store_id via their own add_mirror. A victim node subscribed to that store will, in its periodic loop, fetch the attacker-supplied URL through the same unvalidated http_download. That is a remotely-triggerable, no-cert-required, blind GET SSRF against subscribed nodes.

Exploit is constrained, which caps it at Low: it is GET-only; blind (the response is streamed to a .dat file and validated as a delta tree, never reflected to the attacker); aiohttp restricts schemes to http/https (no file://), so no local file read; and the request path is fixed as url + '/' + get_delta_filename(...).dat, so the attacker cannot append arbitrary paths (e.g. cannot reach the AWS metadata path /latest/meta-data/...). Residual impact: forcing the node to issue outbound GETs to arbitrary internal hosts/ports (internal port scan / reachability oracle via timing/connection errors logged, hitting fire-on-GET internal endpoints) plus a MaxDeltaFileSizeExceeded/size sink. Real but low impact; candidate's Low rating is correct.
verifiers.exploit_sketch: Remote (no RPC cert needed) variant: Attacker runs an internal-network-adjacent or on-chain actor. (1) Attacker creates a DataLayer mirror coin on-chain for a store_id that a victim node is subscribed to, using add_mirror with urls=["http://10.0.0.5:6379"] (or http://169.254.169.254 etc.). This is published via the wallet (DLNewMirror, data_layer.py:942-944) and costs only the mirror coin amount+fee. (2) On the victim node, periodically_manage_data -> update_subscription -> update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:1084, 953-959) reads that mirror URL from dl_get_mirrors and stores it as a server for the store. (3) fetch_and_validate (data_layer.py:624-675) selects the URL and download_file/http_download issues session.get("http://10.0.0.5:6379/<delta>.dat") from the victim host. Result: blind, GET-only SSRF -- victim node connects outbound to an attacker-chosen internal host/port. Useful as an internal reachability/port-scan oracle and to force connections to internal HTTP services that act on a GET to a fixed path. Not usable for cloud-metadata exfil (cannot control the appended path) and not for local file read (http/https only). Local/operator variant via the subscribe/add_mirror RPC directly is self-SSRF and negligible because the RPC requires the node's own CERT_REQUIRED client certificate.

summary: Security audit of chia-blockchain high-value remote-reachable surface: P2P handlers, mempool/consensus, wallet RPC + offers, data_layer. Find -> adversarial verify -> rank.

phases.title: Synthesize
phases.detail: rank by severity x reachability x program payout

workflowProgress.label: find:fullnode-p2p
workflowProgress.lastToolSummary: Scope reviewed (all read with concrete data-flow from untru…
